children.text: Of the sums granted to an eligible institution under this part for any fiscal year, 10 percent may, at the discretion of the institution, remain available for expenditure during the succeeding fiscal year to carry out programs under this part.

Any of the sums so granted to an institution for a fiscal year which are not needed by that institution to operate work-study programs during that fiscal year, and which it does not wish to use during the next fiscal year as authorized in the preceding sentence, shall remain available to the Secretary for making grants under to other institutions in the same State until the close of the second fiscal year next succeeding the fiscal year for which such funds were appropriated.section 1087–53 of this title

Up to 10 percent of the sums the Secretary determines an eligible institution may receive from funds which have been appropriated for a fiscal year may be used by the Secretary to make grants under this part to such institution for expenditure during the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year for which the sums were appropriated.

An eligible institution may make payments to students of wages earned after the end of the academic year, but prior to the beginning of the succeeding fiscal year, from such succeeding fiscal year’s appropriations.

children.heading: Flexible use of funds
children.label: c Flexible use of funds
children.text: An eligible institution may, upon the request of a student, make payments to the student under this part by crediting the student’s account at the institution or by making a direct deposit to the student’s account at a depository institution. An eligible institution may only credit the student’s account at the institution for (1) tuition and fees, (2) in the case of institutionally owned housing, room and board, and (3) other institutionally provided goods and services.

children.heading: Flexibility in the event of a major disaster
children.label: d Flexibility in the event of a major disaster
A
children.text: Payments may be made under this part to disaster-affected students in an amount equal to or less than the amount of wages such students would have been paid under this part had the students been able to complete the work obligation necessary to receive work study funds.

children.label: B
children.text: Payments shall not be made to any student who was not eligible for work study or was not completing the work obligation necessary to receive work study funds under this part prior to the occurrence of the major disaster.

children.label: C
children.text: Any payments made to disaster-affected students under this subsection shall meet the matching requirements of , unless such matching requirements are waived by the Secretary.section 1087–53 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: received a work-study award under this section for the academic year during which a major disaster occurred;

children.label: ii
children.text: earned Federal work-study wages from such eligible institution for such academic year;

children.label: iii
children.text: was prevented from fulfilling the student’s work-study obligation for all or part of such academic year due to such major disaster; and

children.label: iv
children.text: was unable to be reassigned to another work-study job.

children.label: B
children.text: The term “major disaster” has the meaning given such term in .section 5122(2) of title 42
